graphdb_rdf.metric_question: How do the conformity assessment requirements interact when a high-risk AI system is a safety component of a product covered by existing Union harmonisation legislation?
graphdb_rdf.metric_answer: When a high-risk AI system serves as a safety component of a product that is covered by existing Union harmonisation legislation, the conformity assessment requirements are intertwined. Specifically, the product must comply with the applicable requirements of the Union harmonisation legislation, which ensures that only safe and compliant products are placed on the market. The interaction can be summarized as follows: 1. **Compliance Obligation**: The product, including its high-risk AI component, must adhere to the relevant requirements set forth in the Union harmonisation legislation. This is crucial for ensuring safety and compliance. 2. **Conformity Assessment**: The conformity assessment process for the high-risk AI system must align with the assessment requirements of the harmonisation legislation. This means that the AI system must undergo appropriate evaluations to demonstrate compliance with both the AI Act and the specific safety standards outlined in the harmonisation legislation. 3. **Legal Framework**: The framework established by the Union harmonisation legislation applies to both the product and the high-risk AI system, ensuring that regulatory obligations are met comprehensively. For more detailed information, you can refer to the entity related to the applicable requirements of Union harmonisation legislation [here](https://euaiact.eu/resource/LegalInstrument/applicable_requirements_of_Union_harmonisation_legislation).
